sources.label: Fuji Electric news release - Malaysia switchboard capacity expansion for data centers (Apr 28, 2026; construction starts June 2026)
Fuji Electric Global news release index
TradingView/Reuters - Fuji Electric treasury stock acquisition parameters
Note.com - Fuji Electric FY2026 strategy: ejector cooling machine (June 2026 launch) and SiC 3D-wiring modules

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and a +0.4% drift day; the data-center catalysts (4x skid/switchboard capacity in Malaysia, June ejector-cooling launch, SiC 3D-wiring modules) were all disclosed pre-June 6 and were already reflected in yesterday's hold. Rank 36 in the secondary residual-alpha bucket still fits: real bottleneck adjacency but only modest mispricing, with shares now slightly above Jefferies' raised JPY13,800 target.
verdicts.bull: Power semis plus switchboards/UPS give dual exposure to the AI power bottleneck; 4x capacity expansion for data-center skid systems and the June launch of an 85%-energy-saving ejector cooling machine could drive a re-rating as an AI data-center name, with a buyback and JPY109 dividend supporting the floor.
verdicts.neutral: At +15.6% YTD and ~$14B cap, the stock has drifted up with the sector without thesis-changing news; it is neither cheap enough nor hidden enough to be a top residual-alpha pick, justifying a mid-table secondary-bucket rank.
verdicts.bear: Industrial-conglomerate mix dilutes the AI exposure; price now sits above the raised sell-side target, and any data-center capex digestion or yen strength hits both the power-semi and systems segments with limited valuation cushion.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Commercial traction of the June 2026 ejector cooling launch and Malaysia switchboard capacity ramp (operations from October 2026) translating into data-center order disclosures.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Re-rating already largely done relative to sell-side targets; AI data-center capex digestion or weak power-semi pricing erodes the residual-alpha case.
